description_long: This is a traditional ceramic jug-shaped vase with a rounded body, a narrow neck, and a single sturdy handle. The surface is finished in a dark bluish-gray glaze, adorned with hand-painted white script in German and stylized architectural motifs in orange, brown, and cream near the rim. The writing encircles the body, giving it a decorative and cultural character, while the painted motifs add a touch of regional or historical flair. The rim and base are slightly flared, providing stability and a classic silhouette. The interior appears unglazed or lightly glazed, typical of utilitarian yet decorative pottery. The vase shows some minor surface wear, suggesting age or regular use, but remains structurally intact.
description: A dark bluish-gray ceramic vase with a rounded body, single handle, and hand-painted German script and architectural motifs. The surface is slightly worn but retains its decorative charm.
description_view_0: Front view showing the handle on the left, prominent white script, and part of the painted architectural motif near the rim.
description_view_1: Side view with the handle mostly hidden, more of the script visible, and a clear band of painted motifs encircling the neck.
description_view_2: Rear view with the handle not visible, script wrapping around the body, and the full decorative band near the rim.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the handle on the right, script continuing around the body, and the painted motif partially visible.
attribute_pattern: handwritten script and stylized architectural motifs
